page: Microsoft Azure D160as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dasv7 server family, powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 160 virtual CPUs mapped to 80 physical cores with multi-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 640.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable to support external disk attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the D160as_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple categories. Multi-core and single-core CPU tests place the instance in the top 10% of tested hardware, supported by high memory bandwidth speeds of up to 14,657.53 GB/sec for cached reads and 464.03 GB/sec for uncached reads. Cryptographic and integer operations are accelerated by hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier, reflecting high resource density and efficient memory-to-CPU communication.

The absence of local storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents an architectural tradeoff, requiring reliance on network-attached storage and limiting its suitability for heavy graphics processing. However, the high density of dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 640.0 GB of RAM offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale, CPU-bound enterprise workloads. The instance is highly suited for large database operations, multi-threaded data processing, CPU-based LLM inference, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure D160as_v7 is a high-capacity general-purpose virtual machine from the Dasv7 family, powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processors. It features 160 vCPUs (80 physical cores) running at 4.5 GHz and 640.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. Lacking local storage and GPUs, the instance relies on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks place the server in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It also demonstrates strong CPU-based LLM inference capabilities. This hardware profile is highly efficient for large-scale enterprise workloads, including database management, high-throughput web serving, and intensive multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D160as_v7 is a general-purpose instance featuring 160 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 640 GB RAM, and top-tier benchmark performance for database operations and CPU-based LLM inference.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D160as_v7 features 160 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 640 GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density, dedicated AMD EPYC platform delivering top-tier multi-core performance and balanced memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processors with 160 vCPUs and 80 physical cores.
Equipped with 640.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core.
Top-tier benchmark performance in multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations.
Supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions including AVX-512 and AES.
Lacks local storage and GPUs, requiring external Premium Storage.
Optimized for large databases, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
